meta.title: Winners of the Colosseum Cypherpunk Hackathon

transcript:
Clay Robbins: Hey everyone. Uh, I'm Clay and alongside Matty and Nate, uh, we have the awesome privilege of, uh, introducing you to your new favorite, uh, products and protocols, uh, coming out of Solana's Startup Arena, which which we like to call the Colosseum. And, uh, and that arena has gotten massive. Um, since starting Colosseum, we've had the daunting task of evaluating thousands and thousands of projects alongside our judging panel, um, from all corners of the world. Uh, and the thing that sits with us is truly how, um, creative, innovative, and practical, um, uh, the products and and and pro protocols that you guys are bringing, uh, on-chain. Um, and so to get to the winners, uh, we'll we'll get into that in just a second. But, um, before we do, I wanted to just say, um, this is an important and exciting moment, um, for many of those who are sitting in the audience and and watching on the livestream. Um, but also, uh, uh, inevitably a disappointing uh moment for many of you as well too. Um, but it's just a point in time. Uh, a point in time along the milestone, uh, or along the uh the long journey of uh of building a product that is truly going to make a dent in the world. So, um, excited to to get into the winners now. So, starting with the DeFi Track. Um, in fifth place, we have Hobba. In fourth place, Archer. In third place, Rekt. In second place, Cormos. And the winner of the DeFi Track in first place is Yumi Finance. Moving on to the Stablecoin Track. In fifth place, we have Sp3nd. In fourth place, Mercantill. In third place, Cloak. And in second place... second place is Credible. And then winning the Stablecoin Track, MCPay. Congrats.

Nate Levine: All right, moving on next to the Infra Track. In fifth place, we have Hyperstack. In fourth place, we have Pine Analytics. In third place, Ionic. In second place, Corbits. And last but not least, your winner of the Infra Track is Seer. Up next is the Consumer Track. In fifth place, we have Nomu. In fourth place, toaster.trade. In third place, Fora. In second place, Superfan. And the winner of the Consumer Track is Capitola. All right, next up is the RWA Track. In fifth place, we have Watchtower. In fourth place, we have Pencil Finance. In third place, we have Legasi. In second place, bore.fi. And your winner of the RWA Track is Autonom. Last but not least, we have the Undefined Track. In fifth place, we have Humanship ID. In fourth place, Solana ATM. In third place, Plaipin. In second place, Echo. And your winner of the Undefined Track is Attention Markets.

Matty Taylor: All right. Um, so we have a few individual prizes. So the first one is the University Award for the best student-led team. That goes to Pythia. Um, for the best open source submission, uh, that benefits the entire Solana ecosystem, we have Samui Wallet. And then the Grand Champion. Maybe we can get a clap going here. Just across... All right. The Grand Champion is four-time participant in our hackathons, Unruggable. Unruggable is a is a brand new Solana-native hardware wallet and companion app. Excited about that coming out soon. Um, just some last few things here. We have a winners and honorable mentions blog post. It's going to be released right as soon as we get off stage. Of the winners, we're going to choose the top 10 to actually invest in through our venture fund and run through our next accelerator cohort. And then, uh, upcoming next will be uh the hackathon dates for 2026. So if you lost or want to come back, um, there'll be more opportunities to do so next year along with our Eternal program which runs basically throughout the year when we're not running our hackathons. Um, thanks so much. Congrats to the winners and uh, yeah, see you guys next year. Thank you guys.

# Base–Solana Bridge Goes Live with Chainlink CCIP, Unlocking Massive Cross-Chain Liquidity
![Liam Turner](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%2048%2048%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)
By[Liam Turner](https://www.cryptoninjas.net/authors/liam/)
December 6, 2025
* 3min read
![base-solana](https://www.cryptoninjas.net/wp-content/uploads/base-solana.jpeg)
**Key Takeaways:**
* The Base–Solana Bridge is now live on mainnet, secured jointly by Chainlink CCIP and Coinbase.
* Users and developers can transfer SOL and SPL tokens to Base apps, enabling unified liquidity across both ecosystems.
* The launch marks one of the most significant cross-chain expansions for Base, strengthening its position as a hub for multichain Web3 activity.
The long-anticipated Base–Solana Bridge has officially[launched](https://x.com/chainlink/status/1996657107597160887?s=20), creating one of the most consequentia

# Base-Solana Bridge Goes Live on Mainnet
Base users can now move assets directly between Base and Solana through a new open-source bridge secured by Chainlink CCIP, expanding cross-chain liquidity and app interoperability.
[**]()
[**]()
[**]()
[**]()
[**](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https://unchainedcrypto.com/base-solana-bridge-goes-live-on-mainnet/)
[**](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201536%201024'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
Unsplash + AI-generated
[
![Tikta ](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20150%20150'%3E%3C/svg%3E)Tikta
](https://unchainedcrypto.com/author/tikta/)
Posted December 5, 2025 at 8:05 am EST.
**Coinbase’s Ethereum layer 2 network Base**[**launched**](https://blog.base.org/base-solana-bridge)**an open-source bridge to Solana**, enabling direct asset transfers between the two chains.
“To build a global economy, we need to make it interoperable and connected; and being a bridge, not an island, has been a core value of Base since day one,”[said](https://blog.base.org/base-solana-bridge)the Base team in a blog post.
This story is an excerpt from theUnchained Daily newsletter.
Subscribe here to get these updates in your email for free
Users will be able to move assets across Base and Solana, deposit SOL in Base apps and import and export assets across both chains.
Chainlink’s Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol (CCIP) verifies transactions securely, lowering risks like lost funds from mismatched networks.
Base is the second largest Ethereum-based layer 2 network with $12.98 billion in total value secured,[according](https://l2beat.com/scaling/summary)to L2 Beat.
**Posted in:[DeFi](https://unchainedcrypto.com/category/defi/)[Ethereum](https://unchainedcrypto.com/category/ethereum/)[Solana](https://unchainedcrypto.com/category/solana/)
**Tagged in:[Base](https://unchainedcrypto.com/tag/base/)[interoperability](https://unchainedcrypto.com/tag/interoperability/)[Solana](https://unchainedcrypto.com/tag/solana/)

tags.impact_reason: While a showcase of new projects, it's still in the early stages with unproven impact, but can contribute to the growth of the Solana ecosystem.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose the hardware wallet as the semantic anchor because it is the grand prize winner and the physical embodiment of the story's main point: Solana's pivot to physical infrastructure. The Colosseum setting is a direct reference to the hackathon's name, creating a powerful 'arena of champions' metaphor. For the portrait version, I integrated the text 'UNRUGGABLE', the wallet's name, diegetically etched into the device itself. This reinforces its identity as the champion and its core value proposition of security, fitting the 'cypherpunk' theme without breaking the immersion.
visual_idea.description: A dramatic macro shot focusing on the corner of the titanium hardware wallet. The powerful spotlight creates a sharp glint along its chamfered edge, highlighting the precise texture of the brushed metal against the soft-touch matte polymer. The background is a dark, out-of-focus blur of the Colosseum's concrete architecture, with deep shadows creating a sense of solidity and security. NO TEXT.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A dramatic macro shot focusing intensely on the corner of a titanium hardware wallet, perfectly centered and composed for a square aspect ratio. A powerful, volumetric spotlight with visible light rays creates a sharp, brilliant glint along the wallet's chamfered edge, meticulously highlighting the precise, directional texture of the brushed titanium metal. This striking metallic detail contrasts with the smooth, soft-touch matte polymer of the wallet, showcasing every micro-detail and imperfection. The lighting features high falloff, causing everything outside the illuminated area to recede into deep darkness. In the background, a dark, out-of-focus blur reveals the brutalist concrete architecture of the Colosseum, with deep, stark shadows that convey a profound sense of solidity and security. The tight macro framing ensures the entire frame is filled with rich, textural detail. No text or numbers are present on the wallet or in the scene. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, Unreal Engine 5, volumetric lighting, High Dynamic Range (HDR), deep blacks, warm amber highlights, shallow depth of field, studio lighting.

editorial.headline: Hardware Wallet 'Unruggable' Takes Grand Prize at Colosseum Cypherpunk Hackathon
editorial.standfirst: Solana’s accelerator arm signals a pivot toward physical infrastructure and vertical integration, crowning a native hardware wallet as its 2025 champion amid a field of institutional-grade DeFi and RWA protocols.
key_takeaways: **Unruggable**, a Solana-native hardware wallet and companion app, secured the Grand Champion title after four previous attempts.
Track winners include **Yumi Finance** (DeFi BNPL), **Autonom** (RWA Oracle), and **Seer** (Dev Tooling), highlighting a shift toward specialized infrastructure.
Colosseum will select the top 10 winners for immediate venture investment and entry into its next accelerator cohort.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
In a decisive signal that the Solana ecosystem is moving beyond software primitives into the physical realm, **Unruggable** has been named the Grand Champion of the Colosseum Cypherpunk Hackathon. The four-time hackathon participant secured the top honor with a Solana-native hardware wallet and companion app designed to bridge the gap between "hot wallet UX and cold wallet security." For institutional observers, this selection underscores a growing recognition that mass adoption requires a fundamental rethink of the self-custody user experience, moving away from generic multi-chain devices toward specialized, verticalized hardware.

Beyond the headline winner, the track champions reveal a distinct maturation in the ecosystem's DeFi and RWA sectors. **Yumi Finance**, winner of the DeFi track, is not merely another AMM, but an on-chain "Buy Now, Pay Later" (BNPL) protocol handling underwriting and loan origination—a clear step toward consumer credit rails. Similarly, **Autonom** took the RWA track by building a dedicated oracle for real-world assets, enabling accurate on-chain pricing for equities and commodities. These projects represent a shift from zero-to-one experimentation to the optimization of complex financial services.

The infrastructure and consumer verticals also delivered high-signal winners. **Seer** (Infrastructure) addresses a critical pain point for developers with a transaction debugging platform, while **Capitola** (Consumer) emerged as a prediction market meta-aggregator. As Colosseum prepares to deploy capital into the top 10 winners through its venture fund and accelerator, these protocols are effectively being fast-tracked for mainnet viability. Investors should view this cohort not just as hackathon projects, but as the next wave of venture-backed startups likely to define the 2026 roadmap.
